materialitySignals: new customer promise and collection workflow
project recipe cardinality and active-context change
shared web, packaged Electron, offline, and cross-host evidence obligations

dimensions.definitionOf10: The cast-management promise is observable through create, switch, isolated edit, recovery, save, transfer, and active export.
A flat bounded collection is justified as the smallest coherent increment and every adjacent organization/export/scene expectation is decided.
Existing recipe records, graph, archive, folder, and bridge boundaries are reused with exact ownership and dependency direction.
Every CRUD, stale-save, partial-write, deletion, archive, and rollback path defines no-partial-mutation recovery.
Isolation, durability, compatibility, accessibility, performance, offline, host parity, and rollback are categorical and measurable.
All 25 behaviors, affected baseline flows, hosts, migration, performance, rollback, and external gates have explicit evidence.
A fresh implementer can execute signatures, errors, state transitions, UI behavior, storage, archive, and tests without product invention.
Collection, dialogs, exact errors, active semantics, focus, keyboard, reflow, and destructive recovery are testable.
Active export and separate recipe files remain explicit and compatible for downstream users.
Strict bounded JSON, ID-owned paths, no new authority, and fail-before-mutation behavior preserve the local trust model.
Snapshots, conflicts, atomic replacement, stale-file deletion, and rollback preserve complete casts.
Recipe/name bounds and measured switch/save thresholds are numeric with recorded environments.
One-character compatibility, relaxed v2 cardinality, old-reader rejection, archive order, and rollback are exact.
Web/Electron readers and writers release together with inherited external gates and no data-deleting rollback.

findings.title: Duplicate naming and row command semantics were ambiguous

evidence.claim: Initial contract did not define suffix selection or inactive-row commands.

findings.problem: Implementations could generate different names or require activation before management.
findings.impact: Visible behavior and automation would diverge.
findings.remediation: Specified smallest unique copy suffix and exact active/inactive command sets.
findings.verification: Name vectors and actual UI management flow.

findings.title: Name errors and modal recovery needed exact contracts

evidence.claim: Initial contract lacked exact messages, aria state, draft retention, and focus targets.

findings.problem: Invalid operations could dismiss dialogs or become inaccessible.
findings.impact: Users could lose drafts or misunderstand no-mutation failures.
findings.remediation: Specified exact messages, error codes, aria-invalid, focus, draft, capacity, and fallback behavior.
findings.verification: Domain failures plus keyboard/focus actual UI evidence.

findings.title: Schema and archive compatibility sequence required closure

evidence.claim: Cardinality, order, orphan/duplicate recipe, and old-reader timing were initially descriptive.

findings.problem: Readers could disagree about inventory or partial loading.
findings.impact: Cross-host and rollback guarantees would be untestable.
findings.remediation: Specified exact v2 validator changes, canonical path order, validation sequence, and fail-before-return behavior.
findings.verification: Multi-recipe round trip, tamper, and baseline reader fixtures.

findings.title: Pack and folder ownership needed repository-aligned rules

evidence.claim: Initial contract left pack input and unrelated folder file preservation open.

findings.problem: Implementation could copy active selections, drop sibling locks, or promise unsupported folder preservation.
findings.impact: Sibling renders or desktop saves could drift.
findings.remediation: Bound creation to active pack defaults/exact lock, duplication to source state, lock merging, and existing atomic canonical folder replacement.
findings.verification: Domain lock/isolation tests and packaged folder inventory evidence.

editsMade: Defined deterministic duplicate naming, active/inactive row commands, exact validation errors, focus, and capacity behavior.
Fixed host-neutral CRUD signatures, name normalization, error codes, pack-lock ownership, and active source of truth.
Specified IndexedDB stale-row deletion, canonical Electron folder replacement, archive ordering/validation, old-reader rejection, rollback, and performance environment.
Regenerated and validated the 103-record product update graph after each repair round.

verificationGates: Product update, generated projections, review, approval, and lock validate
1–16 schema and all CRUD/name/pack/isolation vectors pass
Repository stale-row, archive, old-reader, rollback, and Electron folder inventory pass
Actual web, packaged Electron, offline, cross-host, accessibility, and performance flows pass
All retained MVP through UPD-003 regressions pass with no sibling or terrain drift

rounds.summary: Triage discarded absent-implementation findings and repaired valid error, pack, archive, focus, folder, and active-state ambiguities.
Closed deterministic duplicate naming, exact messages, command sets, defaults, validation sequence, slot behavior, and performance environment.
Found no remaining material product or architecture invention and recommended locking all seven decisions.

stabilization.productVisionAlignment: A bounded cast collection activates the existing recipe architecture while preserving local-first project identity, terrain, deterministic exports, and recovery.
